---
title: "SHANG Mengyao, Melody"
---

I am a third year Postdoc working at Chinese University of Hong Kong.

My research focuses on child language acquisition through several approaches: (1) analyzing naturalistic speech of children and their interlocutors to identify learning trajectories of target linguistic structures (e.g., verb compounds, noun-modifying clauses, conditionals); (2) designing behavioral experiments to test comprehension, production, and narrative skills in children; and (3) tracking the language development of multilingual children using standardized tools (e.g., Peabody Picture Vocabulary Test, Wechsler Preschool and Primary Scale of Intelligence, Multilingual Assessment Instrument for Narratives). I have studied language acquisition in monolingual contexts (e.g., acquiring Chinese in Northern China), multilingual contexts (e.g., acquiring Chinese as one of the majority languages in Hong Kong), and heritage language contexts (e.g., acquiring Chinese as a minority language in the UK or US). These experiences have given me the versatility to address various practical problems across diverse research topics and methodologies.

To learn more about Quarto websites visit <https://quarto.org/docs/websites>.

```{r}
1 + 1
```
